---
title: ""
page-layout: full
---

```{=html}
<style>
@keyframes letrRise { from { opacity: 0; transform: translateY(24px); } to { opacity: 1; transform: translateY(0); } }
.letr-hero h1 span { display: block; animation: letrRise .8s cubic-bezier(.2,.7,.3,1) both; }
.letr-hero h1 span:nth-child(1){ animation-delay:.06s } .letr-hero h1 span:nth-child(2){ animation-delay:.16s }
.letr-hero h1 span:nth-child(3){ animation-delay:.26s } .letr-hero h1 span:nth-child(4){ animation-delay:.36s }
.letr-hero .letr-eyebrow { animation: letrRise .7s cubic-bezier(.2,.7,.3,1) both; }
.letr-hero .letr-sub { animation: letrRise .8s cubic-bezier(.2,.7,.3,1) both .5s; }
.letr-hero .letr-cta:hover { transform: translateY(-2px); opacity: .85; }
.letr-hero .letr-cta-out:hover { background: #f3eaf0; color: #1a1320; }
.letr-stat-grid > div { border-right: 1px solid rgba(243,234,240,.12); }
.letr-stat-grid > div:last-child { border-right: none; }
</style>

<header class="letr-hero" style="font-family:'Space Grotesk',sans-serif; color:#f3eaf0; padding:96px 48px 60px; max-width:1180px; margin:0 auto;">
  <div class="letr-eyebrow" style="display:flex; align-items:center; gap:14px; margin-bottom:38px;">
    <span style="width:8px; height:8px; background:#ff5ea8; border-radius:50%;"></span>
    <span style="font-size:12.5px; font-weight:600; letter-spacing:.16em; text-transform:uppercase; color:#b3a4b8;">San Francisco State University · Dept. of Psychology</span>
  </div>
  <h1 style="font-family:'Newsreader',serif; font-weight:300; font-size:clamp(48px,8.5vw,124px); line-height:.96; letter-spacing:-.025em; max-width:14ch; margin:0 0 36px;">
    <span><em style="font-style:italic; color:#ff5ea8;">Learning</em> &amp;</span>
    <span>Educational</span>
    <span>Technology</span>
    <span>Research</span>
  </h1>
  <div class="letr-sub" style="display:flex; flex-wrap:wrap; align-items:flex-end; justify-content:space-between; gap:40px;">
    <p style="font-size:19px; line-height:1.55; color:#c3b6c8; max-width:48ch; margin:0;">We investigate how people learn — across formats, languages, and contexts — to understand and improve educational outcomes for all learners.</p>
    <div style="display:flex; gap:14px;">
      <a class="letr-cta" href="research.html" style="text-decoration:none; color:#1a1320; background:#ff5ea8; padding:15px 26px; border-radius:999px; font-size:14.5px; font-weight:600; white-space:nowrap; transition:transform .25s,opacity .25s;">Our Research →</a>
      <a class="letr-cta-out" href="team.html" style="text-decoration:none; color:#f3eaf0; border:1px solid rgba(243,234,240,.28); padding:15px 26px; border-radius:999px; font-size:14.5px; font-weight:600; white-space:nowrap; transition:background .25s,color .25s;">Meet the Team</a>
    </div>
  </div>
</header>


<div class="page-content">

  <p class="section-label">Research</p>
  <h2 class="section-heading">What We Study</h2>
  <p class="section-subheading">The LETR Lab explores the cognitive, linguistic, and motivational processes that underlie learning across diverse contexts and populations.</p>

  <div class="research-grid">
    <div class="research-card">
      <span class="research-card-icon">📖</span>
      <div class="research-card-title">Reading Across Mediums</div>
      <p class="research-card-desc">How does reading format — print, digital screen, or audio — shape comprehension? We examine the cognitive and metacognitive processes behind format-based differences in learning.</p>
    </div>
    <div class="research-card">
      <span class="research-card-icon">🧠</span>
      <div class="research-card-title">Knowledge, Beliefs &amp; Reasoning</div>
      <p class="research-card-desc">Why do people hold — and sometimes update — beliefs about controversial scientific topics? We study knowledge revision, argumentation, and how emotions interact with evidence evaluation.</p>
    </div>
    <div class="research-card">
      <span class="research-card-icon">🌱</span>
      <div class="research-card-title">Mental Health in Academia</div>
      <p class="research-card-desc">Funded by an APF Grant, this project investigates the mental health and well-being of early career academics, examining systemic and individual factors that shape their experiences.</p>
    </div>
  </div>

  <a href="research.html" class="btn-text">Explore all research →</a>

  <hr class="section-divider">

  <p class="section-label">Recent Work</p>
  <h2 class="section-heading">Selected Publications</h2>

  <div class="recent-pubs">
    <div class="recent-pub-item">
      <div class="recent-pub-year">2026</div>
      <div class="recent-pub-content">
        <div class="recent-pub-title"><a href="https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0361476X2600041X" target="_blank">Text comprehension across print and audio: A person-centered mixed methods study of learner profiles</a></div>
        <div class="recent-pub-venue">Contemporary Educational Psychology · Singh, A.</div>
      </div>
    </div>
    <div class="recent-pub-item">
      <div class="recent-pub-year">2025</div>
      <div class="recent-pub-content">
        <div class="recent-pub-title"><a href="https://www.frontiersin.org/journals/psychology/articles/10.3389/fpsyg.2025.1560088/full" target="_blank">Componential modeling of argumentative essay writing from multiple online sources: A Bayesian network approach</a></div>
        <div class="recent-pub-venue">Frontiers in Psychology · Singh, Sun, Alexander, &amp; Zhao</div>
      </div>
    </div>
    <div class="recent-pub-item">
      <div class="recent-pub-year">2022</div>
      <div class="recent-pub-content">
        <div class="recent-pub-title"><a href="https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s10648-021-09653-2" target="_blank">Audiobooks, print and comprehension: What we know, what we don't, and what we need to find out</a></div>
        <div class="recent-pub-venue">Educational Psychology Review · Singh &amp; Alexander</div>
      </div>
    </div>
    <div class="recent-pub-item">
      <div class="recent-pub-year">2022</div>
      <div class="recent-pub-content">
        <div class="recent-pub-title"><a href="https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s11145-021-10241-7" target="_blank">How romanized orthography influences Hindi-English bilingual word recognition</a></div>
        <div class="recent-pub-venue">Reading and Writing · Singh et al.</div>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>

  <a href="publications.html" class="btn-text">View all publications →</a>

  <hr class="section-divider">

  <div class="contact-block">
    <div class="contact-block-inner">
      <div>
        <div class="contact-title">Get in Touch</div>
        <p class="contact-desc">We are located in the Ethnic Studies and Psychology Building at San Francisco State University. Prospective students and collaborators are welcome to reach out.</p>
        <div class="contact-detail">
          <a href="mailto:asingh56@sfsu.edu">asingh56@sfsu.edu</a> &nbsp;·&nbsp; EP Building, Room 319, 1600 Holloway Ave, San Francisco, CA 94132
        </div>
      </div>
      <a href="team.html" class="btn-primary" style="white-space: nowrap;">Meet the Team →</a>
    </div>
  </div>

</div>
```
